pattern: Every maximal run where each day's price is exactly one less than the day before contributes every one of its contiguous sub-periods (including each single day on its own), so tracking a run length and adding the triangular number when it breaks totals them all in one pass.
hints: A single day by itself always counts as its own smooth descent period, so the answer is never zero for a non-empty array.
Track the length of the current run where price[i] == price[i-1] - 1; a run of length L contributes L*(L+1)/2 total periods (every contiguous sub-run within it).
Whenever the exact-drop-by-one condition breaks (price stays the same, rises, or drops by more than one), close out the current run's contribution and restart the run at length 1.

edge_inputs.description: fully descending by exactly one each day
fully increasing, only single-day periods count
a plateau (equal consecutive prices) breaks the run
a drop of more than one breaks the run even though it is still a decrease
two separate descending runs of different lengths
smallest price value repeated at the constraint floor
a rebound in the middle of an otherwise long descending run

stress.description: random walk of prices with frequent small ups, downs and exact-one drops
